grader_module.purpose: Judge whether debate-engine framing, rounds, contributions, and panel behaviour complied with the debate contract.

hard_fail_conditions.condition: The contribution must state one specific, contestable, consequential claim
Questions directed at this participant in the previous round are answered in the current round

discipline.name: Commit to a single claim
discipline.description: The contribution must state one specific, contestable, consequential claim

check.rule: Contribution contains an explicit claim statement, typically in opening paragraph
check.fail_signal: No sentence can be identified as the central claim

check.rule: Claim references specific subjects, conditions, or outcomes rather than generalities
check.fail_signal: Claim uses vague terms like 'important', 'should consider', 'matters' without specificity

check.rule: A reasonable opponent could take the opposite position
check.fail_signal: Claim is a truism or statement of fact that no one would dispute

check.rule: Contribution commits to one claim, not two or more in parallel
check.fail_signal: Contribution hedges between multiple positions or uses 'either/or' as its thesis

discipline.remediation: Return to participant for tightening before entering record

discipline.name: Structure the argument around the claim
discipline.description: Every paragraph traces back to the claim as support, objection, or illustration

check.rule: Each paragraph can be classified as supporting, anticipating objection to, or illustrating the claim
check.fail_signal: One or more paragraphs cover topics unrelated to the central claim

check.rule: Contribution does not drift into adjacent topics not bearing on the claim
check.fail_signal: Contribution spends significant space on topics not relevant to the claim

discipline.remediation: Return to participant with note on which paragraphs lack traceable connection

discipline.name: Incorporate research
discipline.description: The contribution cites specific evidence rather than appealing to unsourced best practice

check.rule: Contribution references at least one specific source
check.fail_signal: No citations, references, or specific evidence present

check.rule: Citations directly support the claim or a sub-argument within the contribution
check.fail_signal: Citations appear decorative or unconnected to the argument

check.rule: Citations name specific legislation, guidance, studies, data, or documented experience
check.fail_signal: References to 'research shows' or 'best practice' without naming the source

check.rule: Citations are specific enough that a human reviewer could locate and verify them. Where the participant is uncertain whether a source exists in the exact form cited, the citation must be caveated as 'requires verification' rather than presented as established fact
check.fail_signal: Citation presents a source as established fact but lacks sufficient specificity for independent verification, or cites a source the participant cannot confidently confirm exists

discipline.remediation: Return to participant to add specific citations

discipline.name: Anticipate opposing claims
discipline.description: The contribution names and engages at least one counterargument before it arrives

check.rule: Contribution contains an identifiable section naming at least one likely counterargument
check.fail_signal: No counterargument is named or addressed

check.rule: The named counterargument is responded to with reasoning, not dismissed
check.fail_signal: Counterargument is named but response is superficial or dismissive

check.rule: The counterargument is phrased as a positive claim with a specific mechanism, not as a conditional possibility
check.fail_signal: Counterargument is phrased as 'it could' or 'it might' rather than as a specific assertion about when, how, and for whom the opposing position holds

check.rule: The steelmanned counterargument identifies a concrete population, scenario, or condition where the opposing position is strongest
check.fail_signal: Counterargument appeals to a generic class of users or abstract benefit without specifying who gains, how, and through what mechanism

discipline.remediation: Return to participant with note that counterargument engagement is insufficient

discipline.name: Address flaws in the argument
discipline.description: The contribution names at least one weakness in the participant's position and explains why the position still holds

check.rule: Contribution contains an explicit acknowledgement of a weakness in its own argument
check.fail_signal: No self-critical acknowledgement present

check.rule: The acknowledged flaw is a substantive weakness, not a trivial one
check.fail_signal: Acknowledged flaw is trivial, false modesty, or a pseudo-weakness that is actually a strength

check.rule: Contribution explains why the position still holds despite the acknowledged flaw
check.fail_signal: Flaw is named but no reasoning for holding the position despite it is offered

discipline.remediation: Return to participant with note that self-critical engagement is insufficient

discipline.name: Ask questions
discipline.description: The contribution asks at least one specific question of another participant

check.rule: Contribution contains at least one explicit question directed at another named participant
check.fail_signal: No questions present or questions not directed at specific participants

check.rule: Question targets reasoning behind an opposing claim rather than attacking the person
check.fail_signal: Question is rhetorical, hostile, or attacks the participant rather than the argument

check.rule: Question is specific enough to be answered, not so broad it invites evasion
check.fail_signal: Question is vague or phrased in a way that allows easy deflection

discipline.remediation: Return to participant to add or sharpen questions

discipline.name: Answer questions directly
discipline.description: Questions directed at this participant in the previous round are answered in the current round

check.rule: Contribution identifies questions directed at this participant in the previous round
check.fail_signal: Prior questions exist but are not acknowledged

check.rule: Each identified question receives a direct answer
check.fail_signal: Questions are acknowledged but answers are evasive or redirected

check.rule: Answers address what was asked, not a reframed or easier version of the question
check.fail_signal: Answer addresses a different question than the one asked

discipline.remediation: Return to participant for direct answers; evasion is a discipline failure that blocks the contribution

discipline.name: Tell a story
discipline.description: The contribution includes at least one concrete illustration of the abstract argument

check.rule: Contribution contains a specific scenario, case, or example that illustrates the claim
check.fail_signal: Argument remains abstract throughout; no concrete illustration provided

check.rule: The illustration actually demonstrates the point being argued
check.fail_signal: Illustration is present but does not clearly connect to the claim

check.rule: Illustration has specific details (service, user, time, outcome) rather than generic scenario
check.fail_signal: Illustration is generic ('a user might find') rather than specific

discipline.remediation: Return to participant to add or specify concrete illustration

discipline.name: Choose the right tone
discipline.description: The contribution matches its tone to the subject, audience, and stakes

check.rule: Contribution attacks arguments, not participants or roles
check.fail_signal: Contribution contains personal attacks, dismissiveness, or contempt for other participants

check.rule: Contribution does not position the participant above other participants
check.fail_signal: Contribution treats other participants as naive, uninformed, or intellectually inferior

check.rule: Serious stakes receive serious tone; operational questions can be more conversational
check.fail_signal: Tone trivialises serious harm or inflates minor questions

check.rule: Contribution is clear rather than obscured by excessive formality or jargon
check.fail_signal: Contribution uses formal or technical language where plain language would serve better

discipline.remediation: Return to participant with specific tone issues identified

discipline.name: Deliver a strong conclusion
discipline.description: The contribution closes with a clear statement of what the participant wants the reader to take away

check.rule: Contribution ends with an identifiable conclusion paragraph
check.fail_signal: Contribution trails off or ends mid-argument

check.rule: Conclusion states specific action, position, or takeaway rather than general summary
check.fail_signal: Conclusion merely restates the opening or offers vague call to consideration

check.rule: Conclusion gives the reader something to do, decide, or consider specifically
check.fail_signal: Conclusion is descriptive rather than prescriptive

check.rule: Conclusion does not drown the main point in qualifications
check.fail_signal: Conclusion hedges so extensively that the main point is lost

discipline.remediation: Return to participant to sharpen conclusion

rule.attribute: unanimous_convergence
All participants state positions within consensus_band
Positions differ only on emphasis, not on substance or recommendation

If distinct_positions from Round 1 was 2 or fewer, terminate debate and proceed to panel. If distinct_positions from Round 1 was 3 or more, trigger convergence scepticism stress-test round before proceeding to panel.
suspicious_convergence
All participants state positions within consensus_band AND distinct_positions from Round 1 >= 3

Do not proceed directly to panel. Run a single stress-test round in which at least two participants argue against the consensus. Stress-test contributions must articulate the strongest remaining case for a rejected option, identify evidence that would falsify the consensus, and name the user population or scenario most disadvantaged by the consensus recommendation. Record the stress-test round in the audit trail. Only then proceed to panel.
blocking_flag
True
Legal, Security, or Privacy specialist raises hard-failure flag
Pause debate, surface flag, require resolution before continuing
argument_exhaustion
Current round contributions repeat previous round reasoning without new evidence or logic AND zero participants reported a position delta in the most recent round
Terminate debate with note that further rounds would not add value
discipline_failure
Participant fails to produce compliant contribution after 2 attempts
Pause engine, surface to user, offer to add context or remove participant
insufficient_information
Debate reveals question cannot be answered with available information
Terminate engine, recommend scoping exercise before retry

round_count.attribute: Simple problem with broad initial agreement needs stress-testing only
Moderate complexity or meaningful disagreement needs full exploration
High complexity with divergent positions needs extended argument
Participants disagree on how complex the problem is; framing must be surfaced
If participant_count > 5, add 1 round to allow each voice adequate space
If participant_count < 4, subtract 1 round; fewer voices need less cycling

deliberative_panel.attribute: True
accessibility_expert
governance
product_management
legal_specialist
policy_specialist
privacy_specialist
security_specialist
Adversarial debate
Cooperative deliberation

Panel members operate in deliberative mode even if they also participated in the debate. Each panel role's ROLE.xml file contains a 'Deliberative panel duties' section defining what changes in PANEL mode.
When a panel member also participated in the adversarial debate, the panel output must disclose which position that member argued
debate_positions_disclosed
For each panel member who also participated in the debate, the panel output states which side they argued
Panel output does not disclose which debate positions its members held
opposing_arguments_engaged
The panel demonstrates engagement with arguments from the side each dual-role member opposed, not merely the side they advocated
Panel deliberation only engages with arguments aligned with its members' debate positions
majority_alignment_noted
The panel notes where its recommendation aligns with the debate majority and where it diverges
Panel does not make visible whether its recommendation follows or departs from the debate majority
flag
Return to panel to add dual-role disclosures and demonstrate engagement with opposing arguments
The panel must acknowledge which debate participant roles are not represented on the panel and assess whether their absence is material
absent_roles_listed
Panel output lists which debate participant roles are not represented on the panel
Panel has not identified which debate voices are absent from deliberation
materiality_assessed
For each absent role, the panel states whether that perspective is material to this specific question
Panel lists absent roles but does not assess whether their absence affects the recommendation
absent_arguments_addressed
Panel notes which arguments from absent roles it relied on or set aside, and why
Panel does not account for how it treated arguments from roles not represented in deliberation
flag
Return to panel to complete absent-voice assessment before finalising
debate_transcript
convergence_summary
concession_trail
convergence_scepticism_stress_test_round
framing_artefacts
Viable options
Options that survive legal, security, and privacy requirements
Eliminate options incompatible with hard requirements surfaced in debate
Ranked recommendations
Viable options ranked against framing decision criteria
Explicit reasoning for each ranking; no undefended intuition
Failure modes of the recommended option
Adversarial scrutiny of the top-ranked option

Panel must address three questions: what are the most likely ways this recommendation fails in practice; which user population is worst served by this recommendation; and what operational, design, or policy cost does this recommendation impose that a rejected option would not. If the panel finds no significant failure modes, disadvantaged populations, or costs, that finding must be stated explicitly with reasoning, not left as a silence.
failure_modes_named
At least one specific failure mode of the recommended option is identified
Panel has not subjected the recommendation to adversarial scrutiny
disadvantaged_population_named
At least one specific user population or scenario disadvantaged by the recommendation is identified, or an explicit reasoned statement that none exists
Panel has not considered who is worst served by the recommendation
cost_of_recommendation_named
At least one specific cost imposed by the recommendation that a rejected option would not impose is identified, or an explicit reasoned statement that none exists
Panel has not considered the trade-offs of the recommended option relative to rejected alternatives
flag
Return to panel to complete adversarial scrutiny of the recommended option before finalising
Conditions for reconsideration
What would change the recommendation
Identify information or context that, if different, would shift the ranking
Standard graders/agent-behaviour.xml compliance chain
Presented as recommendation, not decision; human SRO retains decision authority

audit_record.attribute: Retained for the same period as the decision it informed, minimum 6 years
If the decision is algorithmic or influences decisions about individuals, summary of debate and panel recommendation belongs in ATRS Tier 2 record
Audit records are potentially disclosable under FOIA; no content should be produced that the organisation would not wish disclosed

convergence_check.attribute: After every completed round (i.e. after all rostered participants have contributed for that round, not after each individual contribution)
Compare current round stated positions against previous round
All participants state positions within consensus_band AND distinct_positions from Round 1 <= 2
All participants state positions within consensus_band AND distinct_positions from Round 1 >= 3

Do not terminate. Run convergence scepticism stress-test round. At least two participants must argue against the consensus, articulating the strongest remaining case for a rejected option, identifying falsifying evidence, and naming the most disadvantaged user population or scenario. Record this round in the audit trail. Only then proceed to panel.
Disagreements persist but are now specifically articulated rather than vague
Continue if rounds remain; otherwise proceed to panel with clear dispute record
Positions have not moved, no new reasoning introduced, and zero position deltas reported in most recent round
Continue debate; diverging positions often mean the real issue is being surfaced

defaults.attribute: Substantive agreement on claim and recommendation; differences only on emphasis
Unbounded within session, but panel must converge on recommendation before output

rule.attribute: discipline_failure_at_scale
More than half of contributions in a round fail one or more disciplines
Framing was too ambiguous or participants lack necessary context
Pause debate, return to framing phase, re-establish problem statement and criteria
one_participant_dominates
One participant produces significantly more content or is repeatedly cited by others
Participant has role-based authority that is distorting the debate dynamic
Orchestrator prompts other participants to engage with the dominant claim from their disciplinary perspectives
participants_talk_past_each_other
Contributions reference different aspects of the question without engaging with each other
Framing did not establish shared decision criteria
Orchestrator intervenes to restate decision criteria and prompt participants to align their arguments
position_delta_stalling
Zero participants report any position delta across two consecutive middle rounds
Participants are restating positions without genuinely engaging with challenges, or the debate has reached a genuine deadlock that further rounds cannot resolve
Orchestrator identifies the specific crux where positions diverge and either redirects the debate toward that crux or terminates with an exhaustion flag and a clear statement of where deadlock lies
framing_asymmetry_unexamined
Burden-of-proof analysis was not produced or was produced but the user was not offered the reversed framing
Orchestrator proceeded to debate without completing the framing symmetry check
Pause debate, return to framing phase, produce the burden-of-proof analysis and reversed phrasing before continuing
panel_cannot_reach_recommendation
Panel produces a deliberation but cannot agree on a recommendation
Debate surfaced genuine value disagreements that deliberation alone cannot resolve
Panel produces a split recommendation stating both positions and the underlying value difference; escalates to human for value judgment
